_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Gastric Outlet Obstruction in adults, primarily due to pyloric stenosis from peptic ulcer scarring.

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management
USG Abdomen/ Upper Gastrointestinal Endoscopy/ Barium meal series

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) with treatment details

ppd_preauth.q: Are clinical notes with detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, and indication for procedure available?
Did clinical presentation and imaging confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was the imaging indicative of surgery?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advice at the time of discharge available?

common_queries: Clinical presentation and imaging are not indicative of surgery for Pyloroplasty.
Mandatory imaging (USG Abdomen/ Upper Gastrointestinal Endoscopy/ Barium meal series) not submitted for pre-authorization.
Detailed operative notes are missing or do not adequately describe the surgical steps for Pyloroplasty.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) with daily vitals and treatment details are not submitted for claim processing.

clinical_key_pointers: ADULTS - Etiology: Gastric Outlet Obstruction is the sequelae of healing and scarring of peptic ulcer. Chronic cicatrisation of a duodenal ulcer or juxtapyloric ulcer results in narrowing of pyloric antrum which is described as pyloric stenosis.

ADULTS - Symptoms: Classical hunger pain of duodenal ulcer disappears. It may be replaced by a dull aching pain because of gastric distension. Colicky pain is due to hyperperistalsis of stomach. Vomiting is profuse, projectile, persistent, foul-smelling (because of stasis) and nonbilious. There may be distension of upper abdomen with epigastric fullness. Chronic cases may present with dehydration and weight loss.
ADULTS - Signs: Visible gastric peristalsis (VGP); Succussion splash; Auscultopercussion test/auscultoscraping test.

ADULTS - Treatment: Aspiration with Ryle's tube; Fluids to correct electrolyte abnormalities; Exploratory laparotomy; Pyloroplasty is an option for gastric outlet obstruction in suitable cases; Another option is a gastrojejunostomy.
additional_information.pre_op_preparation: Aspiration with Ryle's tube; Fluids to correct electrolyte abnormalities
